content: Cargo.lock gitignored for binary = builds against latest compatible semver = API breakage risk. mcporter.json needs mcpServers key, not servers.

bash ((N++)) returns exit code 1 when N==0 (arithmetic result 0 = falsy), causing set -e to abort. Use (( N++ )) || true. DEVIATION: Fixed SQL bug in db.rs — correlate_events used l.timestamp in non-FTS query path which had no table alias (FROM logs not FROM logs l). Fixed by adding l alias to non-FTS SELECT.

TCP syslog listener using BufReader::lines() has no line-length cap — unlike UDP which is bounded by max_message_size. A CEF message with no msg= key falls back to cef_str.to_string() on the full raw input, creating an OOM vector from any LAN-reachable host." 2>&1 | tail -3
When CEF heuristic (looks_like_timestamp + contains CEF:) fires but extract_cef_fields returns all-None (malformed body), parse_syslog silently falls back to raw fields with no log line. Silent mismatch between heuristic detection and extraction failure must always emit a warn!." 2>&1 | tail -3

CEF hostname extraction relies on UNIFIdeviceName from the message body, not the network identity of the sender. Any LAN device can spoof any hostname by crafting a CEF message. The detection gate (looks_like_timestamp + CEF:) is purely content-based, not source-based." 2>&1 | tail -3
cef_ext_value returns values up to the full extensions string length with no truncation. Combined with unbounded TCP input (syslog-mcp-zu9), extracted fields can be arbitrarily large. Always truncate extracted fields to RFC-standard maximums before storage." 2>&1 | tail -3
PATTERN: Lazy guard anti-pattern — constructing an expensive value (String alloc via format!) before the condition that determines whether it is needed. In parse_syslog, full_text is always built before the looks_like_timestamp check that determines if it will be used." 2>&1 | tail -3
PATTERN: Positional tuple return anti-pattern — returning (Option<A>, Option<B>, Option<C>) from a function where all three positions are semantically related. Use a named struct or Option<(A,B,C)> instead to make the interface self-documenting and future-extensible." 2>&1 | tail -3
cef_ext_value calls format!() twice per invocation to build needle strings, and is called N times per CEF message. At 1M CEF messages/day this generates millions of short-lived heap allocations. Key boundary checks should use direct byte/string operations rather than owned needle construction." 2>&1 | tail -3
PATTERN: Undocumented extension point — parse_syslog has a format dispatch if/else with no comment marking it as the place to add new vendor format support. Every new format handler silently extends an uncommented branch, making the intended structure invisible to future contributors." 2>&1 | tail -3
bead:
syslog-mcp-a7n
syslog-mcp-390
syslog-mcp-ank
syslog-mcp-crl
syslog-mcp-9ra
syslog-mcp-9vi

content: Used dtolnay/rust-toolchain@stable (not @nightly) to match rust-version=1.86 in Cargo.toml. Swatinem/rust-cache added for build speed. cargo audit installed fresh each run — acceptable for CI frequency." 2>&1

Used numeric UID/GID (10001:10001) in USER directive rather than name — more portable across images where /etc/passwd may not sync. Combined mkdir+chown+useradd into single RUN layer to minimize image layers." 2>&1 && bd close syslog-mcp-ab8 2>&1

Chunked DELETE with LIMIT 10000 per iteration rather than single large DELETE. Each chunk completes fast and releases the WAL write lock, letting the batch writer proceed between chunks. FTS incremental merge (merge=500,250) replaces full rebuild — holds write lock for milliseconds instead of seconds." 2>&1

SQLite FTS5 'rebuild' holds the write lock for the entire rebuild duration — O(N) where N is indexed rows. 'merge=500,250' is incremental and processes at most merge_count*level_count pages per call." 2>&1

parse_addr used to_socket_addrs() which does blocking DNS inside tokio::main. Replaced with SocketAddr::parse() — non-blocking, sufficient for IP:port validation. Hostname-to-IP resolution is Tokio's job at bind time, not ours at config-load time." 2>&1
The discarded iterator from to_socket_addrs().next() is not a bug — it's intentional validation-only usage. The actual CodeRabbit concern was likely the blocking DNS call." 2>&1

Kept CORS layer but replaced wildcard with localhost-only origin list. MCP clients (CLI tools) ignore CORS — only browsers are affected. This prevents cross-site browser exfiltration while keeping mcporter and curl working. Bearer token is optional (None = disabled) for backward compat — homelab users behind SWAG can leave it unset." 2>&1
axum middleware from_fn_with_state requires state.clone() before .with_state(state) because the middleware layer takes ownership of the state ref — must clone before the final with_state call." 2>&1

Used acquire_owned() with Arc<Semaphore> so the permit can be moved into the spawned task and dropped automatically when the task completes. 512 concurrent connections is generous for homelab syslog traffic. 300s idle timeout prevents zombie connections from devices that connect and never send data." 2>&1
Extract inner loop to handle_tcp_connection() helper before adding timeout wrapper — keeps timeout logic in tcp_listener clean and the connection logic readable." 2>&1
Reused config.syslog.max_message_size for TCP — same 8KB default applies to both transports. Makes the config surface uniform.

extract_cef_fields returns (None,None,None) in two cases: (1) no CEF: in the text, (2) fewer than 8 pipe-delimited fields. Case 1 can't happen here (guarded by the outer contains CEF: check). Case 2 is the malformed body case this warn! targets.

DECISION: Used tempfile::TempDir for test DBs instead of :memory: — FTS5 content tables require file-based SQLite for triggers to work correctly. TempDir is auto-cleaned when dropped." 2>&1
FACT: purge_old_logs uses chunked DELETE — test with one old + one future entry confirms exactly 1 row deleted. The FTS incremental merge (merge=500,250) is best-effort; on a small test DB SQLite returns error code 1, so the merge error is now caught and logged as a warning rather than propagated." 2>&1
FTS5 invalid query syntax (bare unmatched paren) returns rusqlite::Error — search_logs propagates it as anyhow::Error. Test confirms callers get an Err, not a panic or empty result." 2>&1

McpConfig.api_token was logged in plaintext via tracing info! macro at startup because the entire config struct was debug-printed with {config:?}. Root cause: derive(Debug) on config structs exposes all fields including secrets. Fix: implement custom Debug for McpConfig that redacts sensitive fields, or use a wrapper type like SecretString.

Dockerfile ENV vars used double-underscore prefix (SYSLOG_MCP__) which the figment config library silently ignores. The correct prefix is SYSLOG_MCP_ (single underscore), with __ only as the nesting separator between section and key. Bug was invisible at runtime because defaults happened to match desired values — no error was surfaced.

TCP connection semaphore limit (512) was hardcoded in syslog.rs instead of read from AppConfig. Low-traffic homelabs and high-traffic networks have very different needs. Root cause: concurrency limit added during implementation without wiring it through config. Fix: add tcp_max_connections: usize to SyslogConfig and read it in tcp_listener.
One-character fix — SYSLOG_MCP__ -> SYSLOG_MCP_. No other ENV vars in Dockerfile needed correction. Verified figment prefix/split behavior matches CLAUDE.md gotcha documentation.
